q: Error Spotting: Identify the error -> 'Neither of the two candidates who had applied for the post were eligible.'
Synonyms: What is the closest meaning of the word 'Ebullient'?
Parajumbles: Arrange -> P: to understand Q: is essential R: the context S: clearly.
Active/Passive: Convert to Passive -> 'The manager will give you a ticket.'
opts: A ticket will be given to you by the manager.
You will be given a ticket by the manager.

q: Direct/Indirect: 'He said, "I have been reading this book."'
opts: He said that he had been reading that book.
He said he has been reading this book.
He told he had read that book.
He said that he is reading that book.

q: Reading Comprehension Analysis: What is the primary tone of a passage discussing catastrophic climate failures?
Cloze Test: The scientist was highly ___ by his peers for his groundbreaking research.
Modifier Misplacement: Fix -> 'Running down the street, the tree caught my attention.'
opts: As I was running down the street, the tree caught my attention.
Running down the street, my attention was caught by the tree.
The tree caught my attention running down the street.

q: Prepositions: She is completely averse ___ the idea of moving abroad.
Logical Deduction: If all Bloops are Razzies and all Razzies are Lazzies, then...
Tense Consistency: By the time the police arrived, the burglar ___.
Vocabulary Root: The root 'phil' in philanthropy means:
Idioms: To 'bite the bullet' means to:
opts: Take a huge risk for money

q: Phonology: Which word has a silent 'b'?
Punctuation: Which sentence is correctly punctuated?
Sentence Improvement: He is the smartest of the two brothers.
Semantic Ambiguity: 'I saw the man with the telescope.' This means:
opts: I used it, or he had it (ambiguous)

q: Parallelism: She likes cooking, jogging, and ___.
Subjunctive Mood: It is essential that he ___ his homework immediately.
Subject-Verb Concord: The bouquet of red roses ___ a beautiful aroma.
Sentence Improvement: Had he realized the implications of his actions, he ___ beforehand.
Error Spotting: 'Scarcely had the minister started his speech than the crowd began to roar angrily.'
Vocabulary: Choose the exact antonym for 'Obfuscate'.
Parajumbles: Arrange -> P: but the conceptual Q: entirely empirical R: foundation is S: not.
Active/Passive: Convert to Passive -> 'Someone has stolen my purse.'
Direct/Indirect: 'Please give me a glass of water,' she said to him.
opts: She requested him to give her a glass of water.
She ordered him to give her a glass of water.
She told him to give me a glass of water.
She asked him to give a glass of water.

q: Reading Comprehension Analysis: What is the primary tone of an editorial decrying political corruption?
Cloze Test: The committee's decision was met with ___ approval from all members.
Modifier Misplacement: Fix -> 'Covered in mud, she watched the dog run into the house.'
opts: She watched the dog, covered in mud, run into the house.
She watched the dog run into the house, covered in mud.
Covered in mud, the dog ran into the house as she watched.

q: Prepositions: The management is fully apprised ___ the situation.
Logical Deduction: If all A's are B's and no B's are C's, then...
Tense Consistency: We ___ the meeting before the manager arrived.
Vocabulary Root: The root 'chron' in chronology means:
Idioms: To 'let the cat out of the bag' means to:
Phonology: Which word has a different vowel sound?
Punctuation: Choose the correct sentence.
Sentence Improvement: If I was you, I wouldn't do that.
Semantic Ambiguity: 'Visiting relatives can be boring.' means:
Parallelism: The teacher told the students to read, write, and ___ their answers.
Subjunctive Mood: I demand that he ___ immediately.
Subject-Verb Concord: A number of students ___ absent.
Error Spotting: 'The furniture in this room are made of oak.'
Vocabulary: A person who hates mankind is a:
Parajumbles: Arrange -> P: and it Q: profoundly changed R: scientific thinking S: forever.
Active/Passive: 'Who wrote this book?'
opts: By whom was this book written?
This book was written by whom?

q: Direct/Indirect: 'I will go tomorrow,' he said.
opts: He said he would go the next day.
He said he will go tomorrow.
He said he would go tomorrow.
He said he will go the next day.

q: Prepositions: She congratulated him ___ his success.
Idioms: 'A piece of cake' means:
Tense Consistency: I have been knowing him for ten years.
